text.title: DR Drill Scenario Library Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: DR drill is region failover, again, every quarter.
antipattern.reason: Library never grew past the first scenario.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add scenarios for db corruption, secret-store loss, identity provider outage, vendor breach. Rotate them on the calendar.

antipattern.description: Drills declared at 'someone notices it's slow'; engineers can't tell incident from non-incident.
antipattern.note: Fix: Per scenario, define metric/log/alert that triggers declaration. Practice by replaying historical alerts.

antipattern.description: Service responds 200; post-drill review finds tables empty.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add data-integrity check + transaction-replay validation. Drill is not green until integrity passes.

antipattern.description: 5 drills run; 0 post-mortems; same gap recurs.
antipattern.note: Fix: Mandatory post-mortem template within 7 days. Engineering leader signs off. Gaps become tickets.

antipattern.description: Real vendor outage; team has no runbook; manual scramble.
antipattern.reason: Library covered cloud-only failures; vendor outages absent.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add at least one vendor-outage scenario. Exercise the real fallback (alternate CDN, secondary auth provider).
